title: core.sicp-streams — MIT-style stream aliases on existing SRFI-41 substrate
goal: Add a `lib/core/sicp_streams.esk` stdlib module exporting the MIT-style stream operators that SICP §3.5 assumes are pre-defined. The substrate already exists in `core.streams` (SRFI-41); this module just provides the SICP-named aliases plus `cons-stream` as a syntax-rules macro. With this module loaded, every SICP §3.5 example should compile and run unchanged.
next_actions: Create lib/core/sicp_streams.esk with: cons-stream (syntax-rules macro), stream-car, stream-cdr, stream-null?, the-empty-stream, stream-ref, stream-map (variadic for multi-stream argstreams), stream-filter, stream-for-each, stream-enumerate-interval, display-stream, display-line, integers-starting-from, integers (= integers-starting-from 1).
Wire the module into lib/stdlib.esk via (require core.sicp-streams).
Add `function_return_types` and known-builtins entries in llvm_codegen.cpp for any new toplevel exports (likely zero new; everything desugars to existing primitives).

Add tests/v1_3_edge_cases/sicp_streams_test.esk covering: SICP exercise 3.50 (variadic stream-map), 3.52 (stream-filter with memoizing delay), sieve-of-Eratosthenes from §3.5.2 producing the 50th prime (233), and the integral procedure from §3.5.4.
acceptance: All SICP §3.5 code snippets in the test file compile and produce the expected output.
ux_payoff: Unlocks the entire 'SICP-reproducible in Eshkol' claim. After this lands, SICP chapter 3 streams content runs without modification.
